Scientists Create Man-Made Cell That Feeds, Grows and Reproduces
Researchers have used chemical ingredients in a lab to create a synthetic cell with most of the hallmarks of life. What does that mean?
Scientists are trying to build so-called synthetic cells to better understand life. But the research is messy and complex.
The cell that researchers at the University of Minnesota built eats, grows and reproduces. Until now, scientists had never mastered the recipe.
Scientists hope synthetic cells can answer basic questions such as how many genes are necessary for a minimal form of life.
But synthetic cells also might someday be engineered to do things that natural cells can’t, like making new kinds of medicine.
